observableCues: aquatic nymph with two or three tail filaments
winged subadult at or near the water surface
final winged adult after a second molt
disconfirmingCues: a portable case points toward caddis rather than mayfly
two tails alone do not establish mayfly because stonefly nymphs also have two tails
limitations: This record establishes broad identification and life-stage plausibility, not a current hatch.
Species-level identification requires more detail than this preview collects.

observableCues: larva may be associated with a case
larva has a pair of terminal prolegs with hooks
pupal stage precedes the winged adult
disconfirmingCues: three tail filaments point away from caddis larva
a winged insect near water is not sufficient for a caddis identification
limitations: Case-building behavior varies and absence of a visible case does not rule out caddis.
This preview does not claim a current emergence or recommend a specific pattern.

observableCues: aquatic nymph with two tail filaments
nymph often associated with flowing freshwater and submerged stone or debris
winged adult remains near aquatic habitat
disconfirmingCues: three tail filaments point toward many mayfly nymphs rather than stonefly
a two-tail observation without body and gill detail remains ambiguous
limitations: Habitat and emergence timing vary by species.
This record cannot establish a current observation, site-specific water status, or fishing success.

sources.publisher: NOAA Great Lakes Environmental Research Laboratory
